auth_access_model: The project page and ArcGIS REST services are public; the scaffold only wraps read-only fetches and does not assume credentials.
data_domains: Columbia Basin salmon and steelhead pop/unit crosswalks

discovery_search.note: The project offers a population query tool and legacy map surfaces, but no broad public search API is documented.

metadata_schema.note: The crosswalk project publishes reports and REST services, but not a modern schema contract.

entity_lookup.note: Population/unit lookups exist by fish manager and HUC6 context, but they are project-scoped.

identifier_crosswalks.note: The source is explicitly a crosswalk project for pop/unit boundary and naming reconciliation.

abundance_assessment.note: The source is about crosswalks and spatial context, not abundance assessment.

telemetry_passage.note: Telemetry/passage workflows are not part of this source.

hatchery.note: Some source datasets reflect hatchery or supplementation programs, but the platform is not a hatchery system.

harvest_fishery.note: Harvest and fishery workflows are not exposed as a first-class surface.

genetics_gsi.note: Genetics and GSI workflows are not part of the crosswalk surface.

provenance_versioning.note: Reports and metadata pages provide provenance context, but not a versioned operational record model.

bulk_access_api_ergonomics.note: The public surface is usable, but the wrapper is intentionally narrow and legacy-oriented.

blockers: The public tools are legacy and unevenly documented.
The current scaffold does not model the full population query UI.
governance_constraints: Treat the project as source-specific crosswalk context, not a universal salmon identity authority.
